normalized_content: GitHub issue: [Feature]: /new starts sessions as if no prior history existed, ignoring existing sessions & memory files URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/48217 Number: 48217 State: closed State reason: completed Created at: 2026-03-16T13:10:39Z Updated at: 2026-04-27T04:36:44Z Closed at: 2026-04-25T03:21:08Z Labels: enhancement Body: ### Summary When users run /new in a workspace that already has rich sessions + memory history, the agent should treat it as a continuation with an existing collaborator, not as a first-time cold-start, and AGENTS.md should codify a Step 0 history check plus BOOTSTRAP.md deletion rules. ### Problem to solve Today, /new only resets the session pointer and writes a summary into workspace/memory, but it does not proactively reload recent history into the new session’s startup context. As a result, even in workspaces with many existing sessions and memory/*.md files, the first reply after /new feels like “this is the first time we meet”: - The agent re-introduces itself and asks “how should I call you?” again. - It ignores the fact that there are multiple days of session transcripts and daily memory notes on disk. - If BOOTSTRAP.md was never deleted, its “you just came online, please onboard” language keeps being injected, which conflicts with already-filled IDENTITY.md / USER.md. From the user’s perspective, this makes /new feel like “wipe all continuity”, even though the data is still on disk and indexed. This breaks long-running collaboration use cases (multi-day projects, coaching, product work), and makes the memory system feel unreliable or ineffective. ### Proposed solution Two main pieces: 1) Strengthen the AGENTS.md template Add an explicit “Step 0: Check whether history already exists” section: - Before speaking, check for: - Existing session transcripts under agents//sessions/. - One or more daily memory files under workspace/memory/. - A non-empty top-level MEMORY.md. - Non-empty IDENTITY.md / USER.md or other user profile files. - If any of these exist, the agent must: - Treat this as a continuation with an existing collaborator, not a fresh agent. - Avoid repeating full cold-start onboarding. - Proactively run memory_search / memory_get to recall recent work (today + yesterday, or similar) before replying. 2) Define a BOOTSTRAP.md lifecycle rule in AGENTS.md Codify when bootstrapping is “done” and BOOTSTRAP.md should be deleted. For example, recommend deleting BOOTSTRAP.md once: - IDENTITY.md contains a clear, stable description of the agent. - USER.md captures how to address the user and their main goals. - There is at least one memory note under memory/ derived from real conversations. - Recent conversations focus on real tasks rather than onboarding. If these conditions are met but BOOTSTRAP.md still exists, the agent should explicitly recommend deleting it (and optionally delete it with user consent). These changes alone, even without runtime code changes, would greatly reduce the “every /new feels like first contact” experience. ### Alternatives considered - Relying solely on prompt wording inside AGENTS.md to tell the model to “please read today + yesterday” and “please delete BOOTSTRAP.md when done”. - Manually asking the agent to run memory_search or read memory files after every /new. Both approaches are fragile and inconsistent in practice: different models follow the instructions with different reliability, and users have to remember to ask for recall explicitly each time. This defeats the purpose of having a documented “Every Session” pattern in the first place. ### Impact Affected: - Users running long-lived workspaces (multi-day/week projects) across channels like Telegram, web UI, etc. - Workspaces where memory/ and agents//sessions/ already contain substantial history. Severity: - Medium to high: the agent persona persists, but continuity of work is effectively broken; it feels like hiring a new assistant every time /new is used. Frequency: - Frequent: any time /new is run in a non-trivial workspace with existing memory and sessions (not an edge case). Consequences: - Users lose trust in the “memory” story because they can see memory files and vector indexes on disk, but new sessions behave as if everything was forgotten. - Extra manual work to restate context and re-onboard the agent after each /new. - Onboarding prompts from BOOTSTRAP.md can continue to dominate new sessions long after the workspace is actually “mature”, causing confusion and wasted tokens. ### Evidence/examples ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c1640a0f-c0ec-48d6-ae90-26c0854df7b8) - Workspace with step-3.5-flash agent: - Top-level AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, BOOTSTRAP.md present. - memory/ contains multiple days of notes (e.g. 2026-03-14.md, 2026-03-15-*.md). - agents//sessions/ contains multiple transcripts. - After running /new, the agent greets as if it just came online, does not recall recent projects, and does not appear to have read any memory/*.md. - Similar behavior observed with gpt-5.4 agent: /new leads to “who are you / what should I call you” style onboarding, despite existing identity and user info in the workspace. - Detailed analysis notes (for reference): [2026-03-15-1415-openclaw-new-session-memory-analysis.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26024541/2026-03-15-1415-openclaw-new-session-memory-analysis.md) ### Additional information I’ve drafted a more detailed writeup (Chinese + English) that summarizes the current behavior, root causes, and a concrete proposal to update the official AGENTS.md template with: - “Step 0: Check whether history exists” (sessions, memory/, non-empty IDENTITY.md/USER.md, etc.). - A clear deletion rule for BOOTSTRAP.md once onboarding is done (identity/user profile stable + at least one real memory note exists). - A strong recommendation that, if history exists, the agent should run a lightweight recall (today + yesterday or memory_search) before answering the first user message in a new session. Happy to adapt this into docs or templates if there’s interest, and also to discuss whether some of this should be elevated into runtime behavior in the future. > Author: stepfun > Date: 2026-03-16
title: /new starts sessions as if no prior history existed
